props.title: Page Layout Demo
props.subtitle: A reference document showing every block type supported by _page.json

props.content: The hero component above demonstrates a solid-colour background with the gradient overlay variant. The 'height' prop controls min-height: small (150px), medium (250px), large (400px), or full (100vh). Add 'image' to use a background photo from the vault.

props.title: Tip: Use 'align: center' for presentation decks
props.text: Left-aligned text (default) works best for document-style pages. Centre-alignment with a large height feels more like a slide.

props.content: The 'text' block renders a styled paragraph. For multi-paragraph content, use multiple text blocks or switch to the 'markdown' block for richer formatting including bold, lists, links, and inline code.

items: Bullet point one — uses accent-coloured disc markers
Bullet point three — use for key facts, not prose

props.text:
### Markdown block

The `markdown` block supports **bold**, *italic*, `inline code`, [links](#), headings, and blockquotes.

> Blockquotes use the accent colour for the left border.

Internal links (no `http://`) open as tabs in the Browse view.

props.content: The 'image' block renders a single vault image. Props: file, caption, width, align, border, shadow, rounded. Without a real file path the image renders as a broken placeholder — replace 'images/demo.jpg' with an actual file when using in a vault.

props.title: Image path note
props.text: All file paths are relative to the folder containing _page.json. Use '../' to traverse up to parent folders.

props.content: The 'gallery' block renders a CSS grid of thumbnails. Clicking any thumbnail opens a full-screen lightbox with keyboard navigation (arrows + ESC). The demo below has no real images — replace the file paths with vault images.

The 'slides' block is a single-image carousel with previous/next buttons and a slide counter. Set autoplay: true to auto-advance every 3 seconds (stops on click). Keyboard arrow keys work when the component is focused.
This is the narrow left column (ratio 1:2). Use it for labels, icons, or short callouts.
This is the wider right column. Use it for the main body text or a larger image. Below 768px viewport width, the columns stack vertically.
Cards link to other files or folders in the vault. The whole card is clickable. Cards with a 'link' prop show an animated arrow on hover. Replace the link values with real folder paths.

items.title: Example Card One
items.description: Short description. This card would link to a subfolder in a real vault.

items.title: Example Card Two
items.description: Another card. Add an 'image' prop with a vault file path for a cover photo.

items.title: Example Card Three
items.description: Cards stack to 1 column on mobile. Use 2–4 columns for desktop layouts.

props.title: Info callout
props.text: Use for supplementary information, notes, or context that supports the main text but isn't critical.

props.title: Warning callout
props.text: Use when the reader must take care — security notes, destructive actions, prerequisites.

props.title: Success callout
props.text: Use to highlight a positive outcome, confirmation, or 'done' state.

props.title: Tip callout
props.text: Use for shortcuts, best practices, or non-obvious advice that helps the reader work faster.
Zero-knowledge architecture is not just a feature — it is the only honest answer to the question of who should hold the keys to your data.
props.author: Dinis Cruz

props.content: The 'banner' block renders a thin identity strip — useful when a page doesn't have a hero. It auto-renders on any page whose first component is not a hero, unless suppressed with "banner": false at the page level.

props.title: Auto-banner behaviour
props.text: If the first component of your page is not a hero block, a banner is automatically inserted before the nav bar. Add "banner": false to the page root to suppress it.

props.content: To populate the auto-banner from page metadata, add a "meta" object at the page root with author, date, tags, and badge fields.
The 'author' block renders at the bottom of the page content area. It accepts name, role, date, and an optional avatar image path.
